latest.title: Results of Annual General Meeting
latest.content:
20 Nov 2025 13:02
RNS Number : 4064I
DP Aircraft I Limited
20 November 2025
DP AIRCRAFT I LIMITED ('the Company')
Results of Annual General Meeting
The Directors of
DP Aircraft I Limited (the "Company"),
are pleased to announce the results of voting at the annual general meeting of the Company held at 09:00 am on Thursday, 20 November 2025 at East Wing, Trafalgar Court, St Peter Port, Guernsey
(the "AGM" or the "Meeting").
The notice of the AGM and proposed agenda and resolutions were circulated to shareholders and announced to the market on 15 October 2025.
Each of the resolutions proposed at the AGM was duly passed. Voting was conducted by way of a poll on each of the resolutions put to the Meeting. This is seen as best practice as it gives all shareholders the opportunity to participate in the decision-making of the Company and have their votes recorded even if they are unable to attend the Meeting in person.
Total proxy votes of 13 were cast at the
AGM representing 79.76% of the shares in issue
. The results of the voting are noted as follows:

company_30s.what_they_do: DP Aircraft I Limited is a specialist aircraft leasing and support company that owns and rents out commercial aircraft to airlines.
company_30s.why_they_matter: The global aircraft leasing market is a key pillar of airline operations, enabling carriers to grow without heavy capital outlays. DP Aircraft’s fleet, though modest, serves a niche of medium‑sized airlines and can be scaled as demand for low‑cost carriers rises.
company_30s.current_state: DP Aircraft has just signed a new lease with LOT Polish Airlines and is undergoing a routine AGM. The company is still in a cash‑burn phase, with limited revenue and high operating costs, but the new lease shows that it is actively generating income and proving its business model to investors.

rns_narrative: DP Aircraft has been busy on the corporate‑governance front: a director has disclosed a sizeable share purchase, a new joint broker was appointed, and a fresh lease agreement with LOT Polish Airlines was announced. These moves suggest the board is actively seeking liquidity and new revenue streams.
The company’s interim report and AGM results are on the calendar, offering a chance to see if cash burn has slowed or if debt has been refinanced.
Overall, the narrative is one of a company that is still fragile but taking concrete steps to stabilize its balance sheet and expand its lease portfolio.

crowd_tape.dominant_theme: Quiet optimism – investors are listening but not yet vocal.
crowd_tape.bull_narrative: A handful of traders are pointing to the LOT lease as a sign that the company can win new business and that the market will eventually reward the deep‑crash bounce.
crowd_tape.bear_narrative: Many are concerned about the company’s high debt load and the fact that it has not posted earnings yet, warning that a further plunge is possible if cash runs out.
crowd_tape.sentiment_shift: The chatter has moved from neutral to cautiously hopeful as the AGM approaches, but the overall volume remains low.

whats_happening_now.attention_check: Google Trends is flat, meaning the public is not searching for DP Aircraft – a perfect setup for a surprise bounce.

near_term_catalysts: Positive AGM outcome – a reduction in debt or an unexpected capital injection.
Completion of the LOT Polish Airlines lease – a signed contract or a revenue announcement.
Any new lease signing or partnership announcement that expands the fleet.
A spike in trading volume that suggests institutional interest (e.g., >10% above 3‑month average).
expect_next.watch_for: Look for a clear bounce off the 52‑week low, a volume surge that breaks the 20‑day moving average, and any positive commentary from the AGM minutes or the new joint broker’s press release.
expect_next.timing_regime: The stock appears to be in the early stages of capitulation – the price has already dropped far below its pre‑crash level. Confirmation of mean reversion will require a sustained volume rebound and a clear sign of financial improvement.

buy_vs_sell.bull_case: The company is trading well below its historic lows – a 70‑plus percent drop. A deep‑crash scenario historically yields 3‑4× upside once the price starts to recover. The new LOT lease and upcoming AGM are potential catalysts that could trigger a rebound. The risk is limited to the current price because the stock has already fallen far enough that a further decline is unlikely.
buy_vs_sell.bear_case: If the AGM fails to improve the debt profile or if the company cannot secure additional leases, the cash burn could force another liquidation or a delisting. The worst case is a total wipe‑out of shareholder equity, but that is a terminal risk that would manifest as a sharp price collapse.
buy_vs_sell.risk_reward: Potential upside: 4× the current price if the stock recovers to its pre‑crash level. Potential downside: essentially the current price (the stock can’t fall much further). Risk‑reward ratio ~4:1.

playbook.if_bullish: Enter when volume spikes to at least 30% above the 3‑month average and the price touches the 52‑week low support. Size the position to risk no more than 2–3% of your portfolio per trade. Place a stop just below the recent swing low to protect against a short‑term reversal.
playbook.if_watching: Hold cash until the AGM releases a positive debt‑reduction or new lease announcement. Watch for a clear volume surge that signals institutional interest.
playbook.if_passing: If the AGM reveals a debt restructuring that increases risk of default or the company files for bankruptcy protection, exit immediately.

ai_insights.bottom_line: DP Aircraft’s steep plunge has left a silent but hopeful stage; with new LOT leases and a promising AGM on the horizon, the upside is asymmetric and worth a contrarian bet.

Historical data shows 4 rallies averaging 380% upside. Current position: +373.3%. Your edge comes from waiting for AI Engine confirmation instead of blind entry.
